zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Newmarch於大約1418年出生在英國越可爾沃莫斯利時，她的父親Robert Newmarch35歲，母親Joan Shirley30歲。她和John Neville至少有2個女兒。她去世於1467年之前在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾阿爾索普 煙雨 吉亞德比，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾。

nameMeanings.name: Newmarch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: locative name from Neufmarché (Seine-Maritime). See   Anglo-Norman Families  .

variants.name: Newmark
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Chayim, Meyer, Shlomo, Vigdor.
Americanized form of Scandinavian Nymark: habitational name from farms named with ny ‘new’ + mark ‘field’.
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Neumark  .

variants.name: Anglo-Norman Families
meanings: 2  Norman, English: locative name from Martinvast (in Manche, France). See   Anglo-Norman Families  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Newmarch was born about 1418, in Womersley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Robert Newmarch, was 35 and her mother, Joan Shirley, was 30. She had at least 2 daughters with John Neville. She died before 1467, in Althorpe with Keadby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Lincolnshire, England.

ko.Althorpe, Thorne, Lincolnshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 알소 위스 케아드비
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Newmarch(이)가 대략 1418년 영국 요르크실르 보머슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Newmarch(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Shirley(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 John Neville(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1467년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 알소 위스 케아드비에서 사망했으며 영국 링코실르에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Newmarch родилась около 1418 г. в местности вомирсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert Newmarch было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Shirley, 30. У неё было по крайней мере 2 дочери от John Neville. Она умерла перед 1467 г. в местности алтаурп вит кидби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности линколншир, Англия.
